name: Jeannie Evans
bio: Jeannie Evans is a Chicago-based attorney and antitrust enforcement lawyer running for the Republican U.S. Senate nomination in Illinois. She has built a career as a lead attorney in antitrust and consumer protection cases, recovering billions of dollars for consumers and working on high-profile cases involving Chinese tech companies including TikTok, CapCut, and Temu for alleged illegal collection of Americans' personal and biometric data. Evans grew up with personal experience of gun violence — she lost her brother as an innocent victim of drug-related gun violence at age 21 — and raised her family on Chicago's South Side. As the second-best-funded Republican Senate candidate (behind frontrunner Don Tracy), she raised $510,861 through December 2025, including $300,000 in self-funding, leaving $298,594 cash on hand. She participated in the ABC7 Chicago Republican primary debate in February 2026 alongside Tracy and Casey Chlebek.

jacket.note: FEC data as of Dec 31, 2025. Includes $300,000 self-funded by Evans. Total raised $510,861; spent $212,267; cash on hand $298,594.

red_flags.label: 59% of fundraising is Evans' own money
red_flags.detail: Of $510,861 raised, $300,000 (59%) was self-funded. Limited outside donor base, though she raised more outside money proportionally than Tracy.

policy_platform: Promote small businesses and job growth; reduce red tape and streamline business formation
Bring down cost of living through antitrust enforcement and promoting fair market competition
Cut taxes and remove unreasonable regulations
Oppose Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson's proposed head tax on employees
Fight crime; support law enforcement; invest in community safety on South and West Sides
Preserve freedom of speech and individual liberties
Reform education to prepare students for trades and innovation-economy careers
Support Illinois farmers and expand agricultural exports
Lead on AI policy: keep America competitive, protect data rights, increase AI transparency
Hold China accountable; protect Americans from surveillance by Chinese tech firms

career_history.highlight: Lead attorney in antitrust enforcement cases recovering billions for consumers; took on Chinese tech companies TikTok, CapCut, and Temu for alleged illegal data collection.

trust_indicators.label: Professional background
trust_indicators.value: Career antitrust litigator with demonstrated consumer protection record — unique credential among Illinois Senate candidates

trust_indicators.label: Self-funding
trust_indicators.value: Majority of campaign financed by candidate's personal funds
